system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

timeFormat      general;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Inlet face (plane x = -50)
    ( -50   0    25 )   // 0
    ( -50   0    -25 )  // 1
    ( -50   25   25 )   // 2
    ( -50   25   -25 )  // 3

    // Mid-plane face (x = 0)
    (   0   0    25 )   // 4
    (   0   0    -25 )  // 5
    (   0   25   25 )   // 6
    (   0   25   -25 )  // 7

    // Outlet face (plane x = -500)
    ( -500  0    25 )   // 8
    ( -500  0    -25 )  // 9
    ( -500  25   25 )   // 10
    ( -500  25   -25 )  // 11
);

blocks
(
    // Block 1: Channel from x=-50 to x=0
    hex (0 4 5 1 2 6 7 3)   (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Full-length block from x=0 to x=-500
    hex (4 8 9 5 6 10 11 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
    // Split edges on the outlet-facing faces of Block 2 to distribute cells
    (4 8) (8 12) (12 13) (13 9)
    (5 9) (9 13) (13 14) (14 10)
    (6 10) (10 14) (14 15) (15 11)
    (7 11) (11 15) (15 13) (13 9)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 3 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 15 14)
        );
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 6 2)
            (4 8 10 6)
            (8 12 14 10)
        );
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 5 7 3)
            (5 9 11 7)
            (9 13 15 11)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 2 6 4)
            (4 6 10 8)
            (8 10 14 12)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

    From function Foam::Istream& Foam::operator>>(Istream&, word&)
    in file primitives/strings/word/wordIO.C at line 74.

FOAM exiting"}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

### Error 1: Cannot Find Points File (Cascading)
This is purely a consequence of `blockMesh` failing — no mesh exists yet.

### Error 2: Wrong Token Type in blockMeshDict Edges

**Error Message:**
```
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 45 the punctuation token '('
```

This occurs in the `edges` sub-dictionary. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the `edges` section contains entries like:

```
edges
(
    (4 8) (8 12) (12 13) (13 9)
    (5 9) (9 13) (13 14) (14 10)
    (6 10) (10 14) (14 15) (15 11)
    (7 11) (11 15) (15 13) (13 9)
);
```

The problem is that the edge split syntax requires individual edge specifications separated by spaces, not parentheses grouping. The current format uses parentheses around groups of edges, which is incorrect.

### Root Cause

The `edges` entry uses the wrong syntax. Each edge must be specified individually as `(vertex1 vertex2)`, and they should be listed as space-separated items, not as parenthesized sub-lists.

### Proposed Fix

**Rewrite the `edges` section** in `system/blockMeshDict` to use the correct syntax:

```
edges
(
    (4 8)
    (8 12)
    (12 13)
    (13 9)
    (5 9)
    (9 13)
    (13 14)
    (14 10)
    (6 10)
    (10 14)
    (14 15)
    (15 11)
    (7 11)
    (11 15)
    (15 13)
    (13 9)
);
```

Each edge is a separate parenthesized pair, and all pairs are separated by spaces (not commas or nested parentheses).

### Summary

| File | Issue | Action |
|------|-------|--------|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | Incorrect edge syntax — edges are grouped in nested parentheses instead of being space-separated individual entries | Rewrite each edge as a separate `(vertex1 vertex2)` entry, all separated by spaces |
| All other files | No issues (errors are cascading from blockMesh) | Will resolve once mesh is generated |

This is a purely syntactic issue — the mesh topology itself appears sound now. The previous attempts focused on block topology, but this error confirms the syntax was never correct. Each edge must be listed individually without grouping parentheses.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace the entire edges sub-dictionary content with individual edge entries separated by spaces: edges ( (4 8) (8 12) (12 13) (13 9) (5 9) (9 13) (13 14) (14 10) (6 10) (10 14) (14 15) (15 11) (7 11) (11 15) (15 13) (13 9) ); Remove the closing parenthesis after the last edge group and place it after the final individual edge'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
